programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. After a run-in with a penniless down-and-out who turns out to be a former boxing champion, Dan and Will patch up their differences and agree to take up the sport. Emma introduces her solicitor boyfriend to her green-eyed colleagues

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat gives Ricky a helping backhander, and Cindy goes to the pool again. Phil's recovery is threatened.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As the doctors operate on Phil, Albert Square watches and waits. Kathy attempts to tell Grant some home truths, and Michelle is unable to talk Sharon out of a difficult decision.

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Geoff is washed away at a fresh water pumping station, and Adam comes up with a novel way for Recall to recoup the money he lost

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew's ex-wife turns up out of the blue, prompting him to re-examine his feelings for her and Erica. Clare's mental condition takes yet another turn for the worse, and an accident forces a local man to face the family he abandoned seven years previously. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Adrian Lukis and Saskia Wickham

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Helen and Paul's romantic reverie is broken by the discovery of a car in a river with the body of its owner still at the wheel. It eventually emerges that the victim's wife was having an illicit affair with her own brother. Nigel Le Vaillant stars

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Darryl's efforts to make up for lost time are continuously thwarted by Sharon and Garth, while Dorien's marital strife seems set to cause further upheaval for Chigwell's ne'er-do-well clan. Comedy, with Pauline Quirke, Lesley Joseph and Linda Robson

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana expose a fake spiritualist, and the Bayview males demonstrate their karate-chopping prowess for Jane. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is reunited with his childhood sweetheart - who unfortunately happens to be the leader of the Communist Resistance, and threw the last man to reject her off the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler decides to provide a service to society by becoming a lollipop man, but unfortunately he does not start on good terms with the locals - a problem compounded by Foggy's decision to give him a crash course in aggression. Rural comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A local writer is murdered, leaving Father Brown delving into her novel Lulu and Lucia for clues as to who might have committed such a crime

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a case originally worked on by a corrupt colleague of Sandra's father, leading the detective to seek the truth about her family's shady past - a decision that puts pressure on the relationship with her mother. Brian investigates a suspicious death at a brewery, while Gerry and Jack take the time to sample its wares

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a man originally thought to have been killed by his hypnotised wife. Brian confronts the magician named in a tip-off and becomes embroiled in a battle of wits, while Gerry, sceptical of the phenomenon, agrees to be put under the spell of an expert at the Magic Circle - with disturbing results. Sam Kelly ('Allo! 'Allo!) guest stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A homeless man opens an abandoned suitcase in a park, only to find the body of a teenage girl inside. Jack and Nikki attend the scene with DS Sally Kirchner and are shocked to discover the victim's baby has been cut from her womb. From the placenta they are able to identify the father - but the married man in question denies any knowledge of the deceased. Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern star in the forensic crime drama, with guest appearances by Morven Christie and Sean Gallagher

programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Ffwrnes Gerdd
programme.desc: Part one of two. In a celebration of Welsh music, musicians get together at Ffwrn in Fishguard to share their favourite folk songs and melodies. Featuring performances by Gwyneth Glyn, Cass Meurig, Lowri Evans, Ryland Teifi, Stephen Rees, Huw Roberts, Kizzy Crawford, Oliver Dickson-Wilson and Gwilym Bowen

programme.title: Sgwrs Dan y Lloer
programme.desc: Elin Fflur chats to singer Aloma Jones, finding out about her career and the sort of person she is at home

programme.title: Maes B o Bell
programme.desc: Performances by artists including Gwilym, Alffa and Lewys who have appeared at past National Eisteddfods, or would have performed at this year's event

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the investigation into the mysterious murder of farmer Frederick Murton in Yorkshire in 1933

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell examine an end-terraced house in Birmingham in a bad state of repair, some land in Rainham in Kent and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent needing a total renovation. The new buyers have ambitious plans, but will any of them deliver their projects on budget and on schedule?

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles heads to Stroud to learn about a little-known inventor who changed the landscape of the world

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia struggles with a request for help, Renee feels betrayed, and Max resents medical advice. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Comedians Ken Cheng, Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Zoe Lyons and Ruth Bratt take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers return to their investigation of a curious case, a poultry farmer who buried the body of his fiancée under a chicken run, but claimed to be innocent of her murder

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore an impressive new build in Cottishall, Norfolk, a terraced house in Bootle in Liverpool and a house with an outside toilet in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the coastguard of Corpus Christi, Texas, taking to the skies with a helicopter patrol and joining a boat crew looking for illegal fishing and smuggling

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Roo Irvine and David Harper help the teams look for items at Edinburgh's Royal Highland Centre, while Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about the sport of quoits

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Efforts to help the local youngsters prove that Leonie and Sully still have things to learn

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sports presenters Mike Bushell, Hugh Woozencroft, Holly Hamilton, Sonali Shah and Jessica Creighton take on the general-knowledge champions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: David and Harry Rich remember their favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including an awkwardly sloped plot given two tiers and a tiny garden turned into a wildlife paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holidaymakers in Greece that left one woman blind and an investigation into the internet scams that have left thousands of people out of pocket. Plus, Michelle Ackerley is with experts as they tell members of the public if their precious jewellery is genuine or not

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Inside the Bruderhof
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a community of radical Christians living a remarkable back-to-basics life in a quiet British village. The 300 residents of Darvell in Sussex - one of 23 Bruderhof communities around the world - live outside mainstream society. They have relinquished money and possessions to live as disciples as Jesus, running an almost self-sufficient community. The film follows three of the residents, including inquisitive 18-year-old Hannah, who is starting to question her way of life. Before making a lifelong commitment, she journeys to London to find out how others live

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a political activist 15 years earlier but, just as it is getting somewhere, Ted orders the investigation to be halted and Ucos is threatened with closure following its perceived mishandling of the Hanway case. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. With Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein return to their investigation of a toxic love triangle, which culminated in the murder of farm owner Frederick Morton

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a terraced house in the London borough of Fulham with no roof, and properties in Gillingham in Kent and Selly Oak near Birmingham

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross visits an unusual chapel in Knaresborough

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Shannon walks in on a couple of surprises at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of experts from the worlds of history, science and antiques comprising of Geoffrey Munn, Tessa Dunlop, Curtis Dowling, Simon Singh and Anita Anand sets out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a property search in rural Somerset, helping a couple invest their £500,000 in a home that strikes the balance between country charm and practical open planning

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich reveal their favourite water features, including a Japanese-inspired pond complete with teahouse

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Richard Hammond, tennis coach Judy Murray and comedians Sean Lock and Trevor Noah join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their oldest case yet, the 1839 murder by drowning of a female passenger aboard a commercial narrow boat in Staffordshire

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers look back at their favourite inspirational gardens. Plus, advice on using climbing plants to reduce air pollution and planting a window box

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Exiled daughter Tosca searches for a roving male to start a new group with, while Flower leaves Shakespeare in charge when she leads the family in search of food

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hi-tech hotel in the self-governing Chinese gambling capital of Macau. Monica gets special access to a secret set of VIP suites dubbed The Mansion, where it takes more than just money to book an exclusive room. Giles visits a special theatre that sends its audience flying through the air, and helps to arrange the flowers for the Chinese New Year celebrations

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Following the wildlife that inhabits the famous American national park, a lost world of deep forests, vast plains and bubbling geysers. The first episode focuses on a variety of species as they struggle to survive the rigours of a freezing six-month winter, revealing that although the herds of elk and bison are weakened by the cold, one animal gets stronger - the wolf

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Judd Trump or Kyren Wilson v Kurt Maflin or Anthony McGill. The opening semi-final gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, with each player needing to win 17 frames over a potential four sessions to reach the final

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The mother of the colony is worried when one of one of her pups goes missing following a battle with rivals the Lazuli colony, which invaded Whiskers' territory. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Mark Williams or Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby or Neil Robertson. Further coverage from Sheffield, as the second semi-final begins at the Crucible Theatre, with eight frames scheduled to take place. The unseeded Gary Wilson and 16th seed David Gilbert both reached this stage for the first time last year, with Wilson coming up against eventual champion Judd Trump, and Gilbert being edged out in a thriller by four-time winner John Higgins

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Lucy is hired to spend a few days with Lord and Lady Repton, but they have peculiar tastes, while Margaret fears she may be reaping the reward of her public attack on Lydia Quigley. Period drama, starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Margaret's debts are mounting as Lydia's campaigns against her and Florence's religious tirades outside the brothel take a toll on business. If Margaret and North cannot find a way to entice new clients, the business seems set to flounder. Meanwhile, Lucy is finding her new profession more of a struggle than she ever imagined. Period drama set in London in 1763 following a woman as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner, starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul visits the Isle of Mull and boards a boat to the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of 'puffin therapy' before heading to the Firth of Forth and Bass Rock

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Mark Urban

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the opening sessions of the best-of-33 frame semi finals in Sheffield, featuring a potential eight frames in each match as the players adapt to the one-table arena

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist and a self-taught metal sculptor create artworks for lottery winners who want to celebrate the day their lives changed

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: The mother of the colony is worried when one of one of her pups goes missing following a battle with rivals the Lazuli colony, which invaded Whiskers' territory. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Summer returns, transforming the landscape of the famous national park from a frozen wilderness into the ideal environment for newborn animals. As temperatures rise, grizzly bears teach their young the secrets of survival - but even at this time of year, snow can sweep down from the mountains without warning

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Judd Trump or Kyren Wilson v Kurt Maflin or Anthony McGill. Coverage of the second session of the opening semi-final at the Crucible Theatre, where the players are expected to play a further eight frames

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Mark Williams or Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby or Neil Robertson. The players return to the table at the Crucible Theatre for the second session of the second semi-final, as the marathon continues in the final ranking tournament of the season

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: There is a sombre mood at the manor when Shakespeare fails to return, seeming to confirm concerns that he died defending Flower's pups in the Lazuli attack. Meanwhile, Youssarian hopes to become the dominant male of his clan

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Judd Trump or Kyren Wilson v Kurt Maflin or Anthony McGill. Further coverage from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the third and penultimate session in the opening semi-final, where eight frames are scheduled to be played. David Gilbert reached his first world semi-final here last year and looked all set to make it all the way to the final, as he dominated John Higgins in the early stages of their match, before the wily Wizard of Wishaw clawed back the deficit and claimed victory 17-16

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants learn about the world of TV special effects as they visit the set of Holby City, where they are tasked with applying prosthetics. After a short demonstration each of them must create realistic wounds, injuries and blood effects, with the two most convincing having their work televised. The following day, the hopefuls come up with a look that embodies their worst nightmare, fear or phobia, before the worst two tackle eyeshadow complete with rhinestones in the face-off

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: A selection of the best moments from the recent series. Sandi Toksvig asks questions connected to the letter R, as Alan Davies and the guest panellists try to come up with interesting, point-worthy answers - all the while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy short, starring Diane Morgan. Mandy enters the local line dancing endurance challenge - only to find herself up against an old rival. Maxine Peake guest stars

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: What happens if you mash together American real-time adventure serial '24' and British family sitcom Not Going Out? The answer is Semi-Detached, a half-hour comedy starring Lee Mack as hapless dad Stuart, that plays out over a chaotic 30 minutes of his life as he bumbles from one problem to another. Tonight, Stuart is late for a DJ-ing gig. The trouble is he has lost his music and so begins a desperate search for his MP3 device - only to stumble upon an angry daughter, a poorly cat and a dead body. Frances Barber, Ellie White, Patrick Baladi and Clive Russell co-star

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Spoof documentary in which reporter Michael Squeamish (Matt Berry) explores relationships, using a selection of archive footage as he gives his unique take on the world of dating rituals, love and modern romance. Advice includes the best way to get someone's attention and what to order on a first date

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the opening day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from today's semi-finals in Sheffield, featuring two sessions in the opening match and one session in the second semi-final

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: With the help of archive, Jay Blades charts how three inventions fixed in the barn - a magic lantern, projector and mutoscope - helped influence modern TV and film technology

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley tries her hand at painting in an exploration of self-expression, while Ashley John-Baptiste discovers how music is being used to give people new opportunities

programme.desc: David and Harry Rich remember their favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including an awkwardly sloped plot given two tiers and a tiny garden turned into a wildlife paradise

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: There is a sombre mood at the manor when Shakespeare fails to return, seeming to confirm concerns that he died defending Flower's pups in the Lazuli attack. Meanwhile, Youssarian hopes to become the dominant male of his clan

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: An early dusting of snow signals the end of autumn, a time when the forests are a blaze of red and gold and the animals prepare for the coming cold. The elk face hungry wolves as they move down from the mountains to feed in the valleys, while beavers rush to defend their habitat against the destructive ice, repairing dams and stocking underwater larders before the waters freeze over

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Mark Williams or Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby or Neil Robertson. Semi-final coverage continues at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, as the penultimate session is played in the second semi-final in the bottom half of the draw

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Judd Trump or Kyren Wilson v Kurt Maflin or Anthony McGill. Coverage of the concluding session of the opening semi-final in Sheffield, where the first player to reach 17 frames will progress to the final, to be played over the next two days

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers take Flower's new pups on their first foraging trip and are caught out in the open by another group of war-dancing meerkats. Meanwhile, two males who want to mate with Lazuli females join forces with Yossarian

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Mark Williams or Ronnie O'Sullivan v Mark Selby or Neil Robertson. Further coverage from Sheffield, where the second semi-final is set to reach a conclusion, and the line-up for the final of the showpiece tournament decided after 15 days of play. Judd Trump held a 14-10 lead heading into the final session of his semi-final against Gary Wilson last year, and the Juddernaut accelerated to victory and a place in the final against John Higgins by winning three of the next four frames

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Featuring a man in Margate who has come up with a unique way of using a shed roof to grow vegetables, and inspirational gardener Sue Kent in Swansea, who shows how she has designed her plot to enable her to garden successfully despite her upper limb difference. Last year, Joe Swift went to Devon to visit a garden where the planting combinations have been inspired by world travel and in Hampshire a sea captain explains how he has created a low maintenance garden using foliage and structural plants

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant looks back at the year when he starred in cult classic Withnail & I, and Rick Astley, Terence Trent D'Arby, Run DMC and the Beastie Boys all hit the charts. TV screens saw the arrival of US drama Thirtysomething, while Damon and Debbie eloped in Brookside

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Rupert Murdoch's involvement in the 2016 US elections and the Brexit referendum, which saw the media mogul rising in prominence after a series of scandals and setbacks, from the death of his mother and the collapse of his second marriage to the loss of several important political allies. The programme examines the resolution of the question of Murdoch's successor and a final multi-billion dollar deal that surprises everyone

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents winter on the lough, with migratory birds from colder climates arriving and painter Lorna Smyth putting the finishing touches to her exhibition of landscapes. At Portora's lock gates, commercial fisherman Eugene Brazil and Roy Shaw clean and checking their nets for European silver eels

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a beautiful summer getaway with rugged brown peaks, clear blue waters and a cosy little cabin

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the second semi-final from Sheffield, as the players look to gain an early advantage in the first of four scheduled sessions, and a potential 33 frames

programme.title: Burma's Secret Jungle War with Joe Simpson
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the mountaineer travels to Burma to tell the story of a guerilla campaign fought behind Japanese lines in the Second World War, which his own father was a part of. Guided by his father's old maps and diaries, he goes in search of the airstrip where the special forces soldiers arrived in the country. However, his journey is thrown into upheaval by current political events in Myanmar

programme.title: Burma's Secret Jungle War with Joe Simpson
programme.desc: The mountaineer's trek through the Burmese jungle faces an unexpected diversion when the first free elections in the country since 1990 trigger unrest, and force Joe to divert his journey to Bhamo, a small town north-east of Myanmar. The hills and ridges where his father fought during the Second World War are within sight, but he is not permitted to go there. Once the chaos settles, Joe and his guide Ed Stafford continue their mission, following a river to the settlements and battlefields where Allied special forces clashed with the Japanese military. He meets villagers who still remember the Chindits, and visits the spot where his father's commanding officer was beheaded by an enemy combatant, and the experience provides him with a deeper respect for what Lt Ian Simpson accomplished

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and Maggie Aderin-Pocock revisit the BBC's archive in a programme dedicated to humanity's ongoing fascination with the planet Mars

programme.title: The Mystery of Van Gogh's Ear
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman joins forces with 'art sleuth' Bernadette Murphy in a bid to solve one of the greatest and bloodiest mysteries of the art world - why did Vincent Van Gogh take a blade to his own ear in December, 1888? The pair set out to discover why the Dutch post-impressionist chose to mutilate himself, and also to identify the unknown girl to whom he is reported to have given is severed ear. Their quest takes them from Vincent's former home in the south of France to the Van Gogh Museum in Amsterdam, and along the way, they seek to determine the causes of the painter's debilitating mental turmoil. The answers they are looking for, however, might not lie in Europe. Instead, a library in California may hold crucial evidence that - if verified - could cause an art world sensation

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford travels to the Hague and Amsterdam to find out what inspired artists such as Rembrandt and Ruysch to create anatomical paintings in the 17th century, and learns how dissecting bodies became considered a suitable subject for art in the period

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the story of the two brilliant but controversial Hunter brothers, who transformed both medicine and art in 18th-century Britain. Their belief that students could only learn anatomy by carrying out dissections created an unprecedented demand for dead bodies - as well as a market for the growing trade of body-snatching from graveyards

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a beautiful summer getaway with rugged brown peaks, clear blue waters and a cosy little cabin

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents winter on the lough, with migratory birds from colder climates arriving and painter Lorna Smyth putting the finishing touches to her exhibition of landscapes. At Portora's lock gates, commercial fisherman Eugene Brazil and Roy Shaw clean and checking their nets for European silver eels

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britain's most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, in a programme originally shown on its 100th anniversary in 2016. He learns about chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend the UK against terrorist attacks and rogue nations

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the opening day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the first semi-final from Sheffield, as the third and penultimate session continues in a match that is played out over the best of 33 frames

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: The Trials of Oppenheimer (2009)
programme.desc: Film documenting the rise and fall of scientist J Robert Oppenheimer, the director of the project that developed the first atomic bomb. He later tried to prevent a nuclear arms race in his role with the Atomic Energy Commision, but his stance made him many political enemies and he was later put on trial for supposed communist sympathies. The events are told through dramatic re-creation and expert testimony, with David Strathairn taking the role of Oppenheimer. Zoe Wanamaker narrates

programme.title: Einstein's Quantum Riddle
programme.desc: The story of perhaps the strangest idea in science, quantum entanglement, the concept that a pair of group of particles that had interacted in the past will continue to act in unison - even if they are separated by millions of miles. This documentary reveals how the idea began when Einstein helped to usher in quantum mechanics in the early 20th century. But no one seemed able to prove the scientist's theories - until 30 years later

programme.title: Rich Hall's Working for the American Dream
programme.desc: Rich Hall explores the ethos of the American Dream, and uncovers how it has been explored and perpetuated by politicians, industrialists, writers and artists

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: In a series exploring national collections at a time of enforced closure due to Covid-19, Alastair Sooke examines Tate Modern's Andy Warhol exhibition. Covering works from across the American artist's career, he explores Warhol's long-running commitment to experimental film and TV, as well as his fascination with advertising, pop music and commerce

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Simon Schama presents a virtual tour of the Young Rembrandt exhibition at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, which features works from the first 10 years of the Dutch master's career. He reflects on how Rembrandt's work is particularly relevant to the Covid-19 crisis, with its portrayal of the fragile nature of human happiness

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A red-hot selection of summer sounds, featuring songs by John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John, the Undertones, Shaggy, Bananarama, Bobby Goldsboro, Bay City Rollers, Fun Boy Three, the Style Council and Don Henley. Other archive sizzlers include DJ Jazzy Jeff and the Fresh Prince, the Sundays, Sabrina, Chris Rea, the Barracudas, Zoe, Martha and the Muffins, Bryan Adams, Girls Aloud and ELO

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage from the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield, as the second semi-final reaches a conclusion, with the players needing to reach 17 frames to secure their place in the final

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me Myself I
programme.desc: Known for her reclusiveness and barriers, the singer has for the first time granted access to her life and music. In this film she recounts her story from Caribbean emigre to being one of the most revered songwriters of her generation. The film covers Joan's childhood in Birmingham, her involvement in the musical Hair in 1968, her musical partnership with songwriter Pam Nestor and the relationship later forged with rock producer Glyn Johns. Plus, how she diversified her style in the 1980s, experimenting with synths and finding a new direction to create music without a producer

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: A performance by the Brit Award-winning artist at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, recorded last week and featuring songs from Our Version of Events and Long Live the Angels

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: Dr Dre talks about his time with Snoop Dogg and recent signee Tupac Shakur, who all became embroiled in a violent feud with East Coast rap rivals, notably Sean 'Puffy' Combs and his Bad Boy Records. This drove Dre to seek out another new beginning and establish a subsidiary of Interscope called Aftermath Entertainment. Dre discusses enduring a string of disappointing solo releases, but Jimmy stood by him as others argued he should be dropped. However, Dre's fortunes turned when Jimmy turned him on to the music of unknown white rapper Eminem

Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Father
programme.desc: The emotional story of Jackie Saleh's fight to find her three children after their were kidnapped by their father in 1986 and taken to Yemen. Police discovered that Jackie's Yemeni husband Sadek Saleh had boarded a plane to Saudi Arabia, but the British government were powerless to trace the children. Three decades after their disappearance, her youngest daughter Sofia, who was a toddler when she vanished, is finally returning to Cardiff with her own family, fleeing the conflict in Yemen which saw their house being bombed. The programme follows the mother and daughter as they are reunited for the first time, as they attempt to form a relationship which was taken from them decades ago

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: David and Harry Rich remember their favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including an awkwardly sloped plot given two tiers and a tiny garden turned into a wildlife paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading for the Elan Valley in search of cuckoos. As he soon discovers they are easy to hear but no so easy to see. He also finds red squirrels that are genetically unique to the Cambrians in a remote conifer plantation, and looks for golden plover, one of the rarest breeding birds in Wales, on the hills surrounding Plynlimon

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Inside the Bruderhof
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a community of radical Christians living a remarkable back-to-basics life in a quiet British village. The 300 residents of Darvell in Sussex - one of 23 Bruderhof communities around the world - live outside mainstream society. They have relinquished money and possessions to live as disciples as Jesus, running an almost self-sufficient community. The film follows three of the residents, including inquisitive 18-year-old Hannah, who is starting to question her way of life. Before making a lifelong commitment, she journeys to London to find out how others live

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Father
programme.desc: The emotional story of Jackie Saleh's fight to find her three children after their were kidnapped by their father in 1986 and taken to Yemen. Police discovered that Jackie's Yemeni husband Sadek Saleh had boarded a plane to Saudi Arabia, but the British government were powerless to trace the children. Three decades after their disappearance, her youngest daughter Sofia, who was a toddler when she vanished, is finally returning to Cardiff with her own family, fleeing the conflict in Yemen which saw their house being bombed. The programme follows the mother and daughter as they are reunited for the first time, as they attempt to form a relationship which was taken from them decades ago

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a political activist 15 years earlier but, just as it is getting somewhere, Ted orders the investigation to be halted and Ucos is threatened with closure following its perceived mishandling of the Hanway case. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh telly. This time he's on a quest to discover the truth about politics and protest in Wales. It's a story of deadly asteroids, MPs on Segways, and a brand new party for the undecided, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience

programme.title: The Young Offenders

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: David and Harry Rich remember their favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including an awkwardly sloped plot given two tiers and a tiny garden turned into a wildlife paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holidaymakers in Greece that left one woman blind and an investigation into the internet scams that have left thousands of people out of pocket. Plus, Michelle Ackerley is with experts as they tell members of the public if their precious jewellery is genuine or not

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Inside the Bruderhof
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a community of radical Christians living a remarkable back-to-basics life in a quiet British village. The 300 residents of Darvell in Sussex - one of 23 Bruderhof communities around the world - live outside mainstream society. They have relinquished money and possessions to live as disciples as Jesus, running an almost self-sufficient community. The film follows three of the residents, including inquisitive 18-year-old Hannah, who is starting to question her way of life. Before making a lifelong commitment, she journeys to London to find out how others live

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a political activist 15 years earlier but, just as it is getting somewhere, Ted orders the investigation to be halted and Ucos is threatened with closure following its perceived mishandling of the Hanway case. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant, and the sight of him in his best chef whites has Miranda in a spin - he has never looked so handsome. Stevie's advice is simple - she has to tell him how she feels before it's too late. But can the clumsy shopkeeper bring herself to utter those three little words? Or will her mother ruin her attempts as she excitedly stalks her poster boy Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Richard Hammond, tennis coach Judy Murray and comedians Sean Lock and Trevor Noah join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Railway Man (2013)
programme.desc: A traumatised former prisoner of war gets married, but continues to suffer nightmares about his experiences. He is unable to talk to his wife about his time in captivity, so she tracks down a fellow hostage to learn the truth - and discovers the cruel Japanese officer he is fixated on is still alive. Fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Nicole Kidman and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the investigation into the mysterious murder of farmer Frederick Murton in Yorkshire in 1933

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell examine an end-terraced house in Birmingham in a bad state of repair, some land in Rainham in Kent and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent needing a total renovation. The new buyers have ambitious plans, but will any of them deliver their projects on budget and on schedule?

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles heads to Stroud to learn about a little-known inventor who changed the landscape of the world

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia struggles with a request for help, Renee feels betrayed, and Max resents medical advice. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Comedians Ken Cheng, Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Zoe Lyons and Ruth Bratt take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Hampshire to help a couple find a permanent country home where they can finally kick back and relax after years of relocating because of work. Nicki also meets a master saddler and her daughter who are key to the survival of this traditional craft, and then takes the reins and tries riding side-saddle for the first time

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some of the most eco-friendly garden transformations, including a garden featuring a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at a unique driving centre in Cardiff where staff decided if higher-risk drivers who have lost their licence through illness or accident are safe to be back behind the wheel

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following a 60-year-old woman who takes on a a record-breaking attempt to row solo across the Atlantic Ocean, which will make her the oldest woman to do so, while back home, her sister is treated for a rare terminal cancer. The programme explores the mental and physical challenges both face

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A budding influencer and hard-working hairdresser move into the apartments, and Jake starts to question his hard-partying lifestyle after a two-day bender

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. The review looked into how the health system responded to concerns raised by patients about the Primodos hormone pregnancy test, the drug sodium valproate and surgical mesh, from Thursday 9 July

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In times of crisis, we learn about who we really are, and so it is that the global coronavirus pandemic is revealing truths about humankind and how we balance self and collective interest. Stephen Sackur speaks to writer and historian Rutger Bregman whose book Humankind: A Hopeful History is making waves around the world. Do we humans massively underestimate our capacity to change things for the better? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Wednesday 27 May.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?
