programme.desc: Tha Bing ‘s a charaidean a cluich geama spòrsail ‘s iad a’ leigeil orra gur e rannsachairean-nàdar a th’ annta ‘s iad a’ lorg rudan le dathan sònraichte. Ge-tà, chan eil Bing a’ cumail sùil ro gheur air na tha e a’ togail…! Bing and his friends are playing a really fun game where they pretend to be nature explorers as they look for things in the garden that match their colour wheel. However, Bing doesn’t pay quite enough attention to the things he is picking up!

programme.desc: Documentary following the activities of Carl, a week-old Nile crocodile who lives with his 30 brothers and sisters in Zambia's Luangwa River

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Morag and Neil teach dancing and the next generation of golfers get a lesson on Askernish Golf course from old hands Paddy and John Archie

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A look back to the US Civil Rights movement, featuring a Ku Klux Klan march and Martin Luther King's famous I Have a Dream Speech

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Tthe challenges of winter on the croft as Sweeny contends with 2018's infamous Beast from the East and some really cold weather. As a full time crofter, Sweeny is also constantly exploring new ideas to ensure a steady income throughout the year. One of these new ideas leads him to a new hobby - knitting - as he heads to the Isle of Skye to get creative with his wool

programme.title: Na Boireannaich a Thog Glasgow City
programme.desc: Documentary about Carol Anne Stewart and Laura Montgomery, the founders of Scotland's most successful women's football team

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads head out of the kitchen to the countryside. On their return Roddy shows how to make roasted Pork Belly on a bed of mustard mash, accompanied by honey roasted carrots. Uisdean prepares a leg of lamb coated in Moroccan inspired spices accompanied by couscous, chickpeas

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Celebrating the people who live and work on some of the beautiful islands of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in Cuan Sound, a narrow channel in Argyll, separating Seil Island and Luing

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Murray MacLeod tries out two of the best SUVs money can buy to establish whether spending more is really worth it. Donnie Mackay drives a car he's wanted to drive ever since it was announced - the Ford Focus RS

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo learns about metal while playing with a suit of armour

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo learns about stone when they carve a statue

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: A bee becomes trapped in Bernie's grotto, too weak to escape and make his way back to the hive. Oona and Baba are on hand to find and bring him the food he needs to build his strenght back up

programme.title: Botannan Araid Uilleim (William's Wish Wellingtons)
programme.desc: Adventures for younger viewers, with the boy and his magic boots

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Al and Bob learn a new game, but are not convinced it's as good as their old sport

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: The friends find a field of wishing-puffs, but Ellie soon needs to sneeze

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Cedric the pigeon flies off-course and it falls to Pàdraig to rescue him from Ireland

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Niall Iain Macdonald reads Norton agus Alpha, written and illustrated by Kristyna Litten and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: After landing a job at Otto's cafe, Luke learns a few unpleasant truths including the fact the 'special sauce' might actually be dangerous for his unsuspecting customers

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Tha tòrr plòigh a’ tachairt ‘s iad a’ putadh na cloiche an-diugh. There’s lots of fun happening as they shot-put today.

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Murdo from Glasgow visits the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow to talk about his favourite hobby, cycling

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: A boy from Japan who is a very strong swimmer wants to learn how to free-dive without the aid of an air tank

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Music by Cathy Ann MacPhee and Maggie MacInnes at the 2015 Trad Awards

programme.title: Bonn Còmhraidh
programme.desc: Donald Morrison takes a nostalgic look back through the archives of the first Gaelic current affairs TV series broadcast in the 1970s. Martin MacDonald reports from Uist on the impact the Army was having on the community, Roddy John MacLeod spends time on Raasay and reports on Dr Green's relationship with the island, the West Highland Free Press commemorates its fifth birthday and there is a report on tourism in Mull

programme.title: Ceol Aig A Chaisteal
programme.desc: Traditional tunes from some of Scotland's leading young musicians, performed at Tulloch Castle, Dingwall

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Peter the inventor is sent on a journey after the locals notice his idleness

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Sweeny questions his future as a full time crofter. As he gets organised for an essential day trip to Uist, he shares his thoughts on how he feels about making the journey

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in Cuan Sound, a narrow channel in Argyll, separating Seil Island and Luing

programme.title: Blas
programme.desc: The best of Skipinnish from the Blas Music Festival in Inverness in 2018, along with special guest Rachel Walker

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Llandudno, where James Layte seriously undervalues some cutlery, and a Chinese wall plaque sells for a huge profit at auction

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five colleagues from a dental practice in Birmingham calling themselves the Wisdom Teeth answer questions on Christmas films, Italian cities and Take That singles. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Tom, Julie, Marie and Ian pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian travels across South Africa and Zambia helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into the wild. As the trip begins he calls at a penguin sanctuary, visits an elephant nursery - where he helps to bottle-feed and wash a six-month-old calf - and takes some lion cubs for a walk at a rehabilitation centre

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: West Ham player Bobby leads England to World Cup glory at Wembley and he and Tina are reunited amidst the raucous celebrations. Fame and fortune follow as the couple become icons of their time, and when baby Dean arrives Tina thinks she has it all. The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Lionel Richie talks about growing up in Alabama against a background of racial segregation and his rise to fame after a string of hits with the Commodores and as a solo artist. He also opens up to Piers about his two failed marriages and adopting daughter Nicole, who he supported through drugs rehabilitation

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives at the vineyards of Sonoma County, where he visits a winery, and a farm and restaurant run by a couple whose culinary passion leaves James overwhelmed. He also meets a Tibetan artist who is undertaking a breathtaking five-year ceremonial painting

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice. Presented by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his dealers are in Manchester, where the visitors include a five-year-old girl who holds her own against Simon Schneider, while David Ford tries to win some Moorcroft pottery

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five colleagues from Gateshead, Tyne and Wear, calling themselves the Canny Banters answer questions on Hitchcock films, US states and football. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Darren, Jordanna, Margaret and Ryan pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps, cute kids and practical jokes, including a car bonnet flying open on a motorway, a teenager terrified by a seagull and a toddler catching out her family with a classic gag - fake poo

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and the experts are in Manchester with the Marshalls, whose eating and spending habits have spiralled out of control, discovering how they are stockpiling meat in their freezer yet spending hundreds in the supermarket

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher reaches Pembrokeshire, where he meets a family who have moved to the coast to live off-grid and takes part in an archeological dig to unearth the secrets of St Patrick's Chapel. He also joins members of Pembrokeshire Blind Society to find out how they use sound to experience the coast, and sees baby seals at Castlemartin military firing range

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals the secrets of traditional Lancashire Eccles cakes, using a recipe passed from father to son, jam fit for the Queen's breakfast table and gin infused with exotic natural ingredients at a hi-tech distillery. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The last of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter centres on an uncomfortable conversation between mixed-race Jordan, who has recently had a baby with a black man, and her mother Lyndsey. As things progress, they are forced to confront and evaluate their relationship as a seemingly immovable wedge appears between them. Written by and starring Nicole Lecky, with Amanda Abbington

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress presents a final selection of previously unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, revealing unseen stories and special moments of how these journeys are made. This one follows her adventures in and around Iran, from where she followed the Silk Road through Azerbaijan and Georgia, finally moving on to Venice. The programme also presents behind the scenes footage from Joanna's travels in Haiti and Cuba, including previously unseen footage of her other adventures in the Caribbean

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by antiques dealers Tony Geering, James Layte, Corrie Jeffrey and Alison Chapman in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Tony blows someone else's trumpet, James gets danced out of his dosh, Alison drops thousands on a claret jug, while Corrie gets her hand on a sparkling solitaire ring

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quizzy McQuizface, a team of pub quizzers from Scotland, answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: American Gangster (2007)
programme.desc: The story of 1970s drug kingpin Frank Lucas, whose innovative methods of smuggling heroin into the US made him one of the leading figures in the New York underworld. A detective is assigned to bring Lucas's empire down, but his investigation is undermined by police corruption. Ridley Scott's fact-based crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Russell Crowe, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Josh Brolin

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay presents the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.desc: Quizzy McQuizface, a team of pub quizzers from Scotland, answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: American Gangster (2007)
programme.desc: The story of 1970s drug kingpin Frank Lucas, whose innovative methods of smuggling heroin into the US made him one of the leading figures in the New York underworld. A detective is assigned to bring Lucas's empire down, but his investigation is undermined by police corruption. Ridley Scott's fact-based crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Russell Crowe, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Josh Brolin

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay presents the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is furious with her father for attacking Chris, and Frank tells the Glovers that he wants to make amends for Chris's treatment of Linda. The police look for a missing Kim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police speak to Frank about Kim's disappearance. Tom is given the job in Aberystwyth but tells Kelly she cannot come with him. Eric warns Rachel to keep out of his affairs

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek convinces himself that Norris has murdered Angela. Liz and Jim walk in on Vicky's farewell party, and Betty gives her the hot pot recipe as a leaving present

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts are alarmed when their door opens and Terry Duckworth walks in with Tommy. Vera is thrilled to see her grandson, but apprehensive about Terry's return

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.desc: The Crawleys watch Henry compete in a motor racing event, an occasion that leaves Mary reeling. Violet hatches a plan which surprises the family and delights Miss Denker, and learns more about Amelia's intentions in befriending Isobel. Molesley and Daisy rise to the challenges of examination day, Andy's secret is brought to light, Thomas feels isolated, and Mrs Patmore and Mrs Hughes teach Carson a lesson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel promised the chaos of her personal life was all in the past, but she is conflicted upon discovering her mother's new boyfriend is a violent domestic abuser. Meanwhile, Janet gets cold feet when she goes on her first date, terrified at the idea of having to impress a stranger. The crimebusters are united in the search for the killer of Rich Hutchings, who is found murdered in the flat he shares with his husband Adam. Barry, their outspoken and violent homophobic neighbour, is the prime suspect. However, as Adam's friend Tim struggles to recall the events of their night out, it seems Barry may be in the clear. Meanwhile, Rachel is horrified when her mum Sharon turns up asking for help. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is certain Terry is up to no good, Alma tries to keep out of Stephen's way, and Rita and Mavis are unnerved as they realise Norris could be a murderer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Stephen tells Alma he does not want her to keep avoiding him. Mavis feels awful for having thought Norris a murderer, and Derek is relieved when Angela phones to speak to Cole

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police are still searching Home Farm for clues about Kim. Frank is furious that the police still suspect him. Terry is bored with Viv talking about Kelly and Vic

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police find Kim's car in the water at the quarry with a woman's body inside, and Frank is called to identify it. Eric announces his engagement to Dee in the Woolpack

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dee is sad that her family cannot attend the wedding. Biff and Linda enjoy a night away in a hotel for Linda's birthday. Zak, Barry and Lisa get drunk together in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles check the damage caused by the rocket explosion, Zak is upset by how much time Lisa is spending with Barry, and Frank helps DI Cooke with her investigations

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma feels Mike cares more for the business than her. Jack feels Terry is only visiting because of their inheritance money. Norris is reconciled with Angela

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera wants to invite Terry to stay permanently, and he is interested to learn Jack has shares in a racehorse. Don is furious to hear Josie has resigned

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's birthday celebrations give her the blues, and even Wilfred's arrival doesn't make her any happier

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Bertie's circumstances change suddenly, leaving Edith uncertain whether to risk telling him the truth or keep her past a secret at all costs. Mary wonders if she can ever make peace with her sister as hostilities escalate, Spratt shows some hidden talents and Molesley's first day as a teacher does not go according to plan. Mrs Patmore's bed and breakfast attracts unwanted attention, leaving her in need of the Crawleys' help

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with excitement as the final preparations are made for the Doc and Louisa's big day. However, Martin is irritated by PC Penhale, who wrongly presumes he is best man - and to make matters worse, the bride is late. This isn't the first time the couple have tried to get married - will the villagers have to put the confetti back in the box again? Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star, with John Marquez, Eileen Atkins and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony is glad Steve is in prison as he was always bothering Fiona. Andy decides to see Anne's parents, and Audrey thinks Alma wants to get her claws into Stephen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis hates having Norris to stay, and Raquel tries to drum up trade but no one wants a massage. Steve is thrilled when Fiona visits him and tells her he is sorry for everything

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned confronts Chris with a loaded gun when he hears he attempted to rape Linda. She tells Biff that her dad knows about Chris and they head off to find them

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is furious with her father for attacking Chris, and Frank tells the Glovers that he wants to make amends for Chris's treatment of Linda. The police look for a missing Kim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police speak to Frank about Kim's disappearance. Tom is given the job in Aberystwyth but tells Kelly she cannot come with him. Eric warns Rachel to keep out of his affairs

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek convinces himself that Norris has murdered Angela. Liz and Jim walk in on Vicky's farewell party, and Betty gives her the hot pot recipe as a leaving present

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts are alarmed when their door opens and Terry Duckworth walks in with Tommy. Vera is thrilled to see her grandson, but apprehensive about Terry's return

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's divorce finally comes through, and he wastes no time in proposing to Miss Jones - but makes the mistake of asking his low-life brother Ron to be best man. Final episode of Eric Chappell's classic comedy, which finally reveals Rigsby's Christian name. Leonard Rossiter stars

programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: An experienced US marine sergeant returns to a former unit to help train a squad of raw recruits and prepare them for deployment to war zones. Action adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Marsha Mason, Everett McGill, Moses Gunn, Mario Van Peebles, and Bo Svenson

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: An engineer hatches a plan to immortalise people by freezing them and bringing them back to life, and decides Simon Templar would make the ideal guinea pig

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. In preparation for an invasion of Wales, King John orders all food from the local community to be collected in order to feed his army. Robin and his band are unwilling to stand by and let their people starve, and form a resistance with the villagers of Wickham

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Drug dealers kidnap a woman's child in a bid to deter her from testifying against them at a forthcoming - forcing Dempsey to turn to shady character the Bogeyman for help

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur employs a truck-load of ex-cons as cheap labour for his new landscape gardening business and things go swimmingly - until a series of thefts puts paid to the enterprise and he finds himself out of pocket. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley is tasked with finding out whether a woman is crying wolf with her tales of being terrorised by a masked stalker. Suspicion falls on one of her work colleagues, an inveterate human rights campaigner, who keeps pictures of her in his flat. Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is called in to catch a gunman who has stolen cash from a lock-up. Hard-hitting detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents coverage of stage three, which featured a 157km route from Corenc to Saint-Martin-de-Belleville. With commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove look to make a profit on automobile memorabilia at the Bristol Classic Car Show

programme.title: Bullitt (1968)
programme.desc: A tough police detective is assigned to protect a Mob witness for 48 hours, only for hitmen to find their hiding place and try to silence the informant. The rebellious cop realises something does not add up and goes in search of both the killers and whoever told them where to locate their target - but an ambitious politician wants him off the case. Thriller, starring Steve McQueen, Jacqueline Bisset, Robert Vaughn and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The doctor puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. It is week two of their very low-calorie diet and the volunteers' tempers are beginning to fray, but Michael gets them doing high-intensity work-outs and yoga. He also explores the connection between obesity and inflammation and the role it is thought to have in almost every major disease and illness, and Michael's wife, author Dr Clare Bailey, shares recipes and food tips that help to build and maintain muscle

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Until it closed in 1966 the Deeside Railway followed the River Dee upstream from Aberdeen into what is now the Cairngorms National Park. As luck would have it, Queen Victoria purchased Balmora just as the line was set to open, giving the route prestige. Rob Bell retraces the route, visiting a shooting estate, tossing a caber and finding an unmistakable Highland 'brand' that all owe their existence to this royal railway age. He also explores a quiet valley of cattle farming and timber sawmills, and, in truth, a simple railway that struggled for decades to justify its own existence, despite its royal veneer

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice

programme.title: Diana: Her Last Summer
programme.desc: First-hand accounts from inside Diana, Princess of Wales' household, including from butler Paul Burrell, bodyguard Ken Wharfe, private secretary Patrick Jephson and favourite driver and bodyguard Colin Tebbutt, provide intimate details of life at Kensington Palace for Diana in the final three months of her life

programme.title: William & Kate: Too Good to Be True?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the relationship between the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge. The programme examines the lifelong preparation William has gone through for his eventual role as king and how public attitudes to the couple have shifted over the years, as well as considering what their eventual reign will be like

programme.title: When Celeb Double Acts Fall Out
programme.desc: A look at how entertainment partnerships have succumbed to the pressure of being in the spotlight, including the likes of Cannon and Ball, Simon and Garfunkel, David Baddiel and Rob Newman, and Kim Woodburn and Aggie MacKenzie. Drawing on archive, first-person testimonies and interviews with performers' friends, peers and fellow industry professionals, this programme looks at how some of the biggest and most popular double acts failed to last the course

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The tale of an elderly couple whose hopes of a peaceful retirement were ruined when their tenants stopped paying rent, then moved in their abusive friends who became squatters. With the house becoming a free-for-all and in a terrible state of repair, and the couple facing a barrage of verbal threats and abusive behaviour, they have been forced to seek help in an attempt to get their property back. Plus, a man who cannot sell his house because his sitting tenant refuses to leave

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels through Argentina, starting his journey at British-built railway station in Buenos Aires and taking an overnight train to the pampas lowlands. In the major port of Bahia Blanca, Chris meets four veterans of the Falklands War and finds out what the locals now think about the British, before ending his journey by riding La Trochita, known in English as the Old Patagonian Express

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: The documentary visits a waste facility in Nottingham which offers an alternative to landfill, burning rubbish to create energy for surrounding homes. It also follows the work of a Glasgow father and son who recycle some of the hundreds of thousands of tons of clothing thrown out every year and the team at Motorpoint Arena in Nottingham, cleaning up after a heavy metal gig

programme.title: Diana: Queen of Hearts?
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at Diana, Princess of Wales' devotion to charity work and good causes, including the moment in 1987 when she made headlines by shaking hands with an Aids patient. But was her compassion a genuine attempt to change the world or a push to change her public image? Former private secretary Patrick Jephson, butler Paul Burrell and bodyguard Ken Wharfe are among those exploring the nature of Diana's charity work and her fragile state of mind as her marriage disintegrated

programme.title: Meghan & Harry: The New Revelations
programme.desc: A look at the events that led to the Duke and Duchess of Sussex stepping back from the royal family, with the couple growing frustrated with a pecking order that left them overshadowed by others. This documentary delves into new claims and explores the speculation contained in recent books about the royals, testing their credibility and voracity and trying to unravel what actually happened

programme.title: QE2: The World's Most Luxurious Hotel
programme.desc: The ship's most important restaurant undergoes a significant renovation. However, time is short to update the venue to suit the needs of modern visitors and chef Dino and his team are on their eighth tasting for the new menu - which still has not satisfied the owners. Issues arise with custom-made furniture for the ship's biggest suites, and the arrival of the replacement pieces disrupts a promotional photo shoot

programme.title: Inside Britain's Biggest Mosque
programme.desc: Meera Syal narrates a documentary going behind the scenes at the Baitul Futuh mosque in Morden, south London, home to Britain's 30,000-strong Ahmadi Muslim community. Plus, Craig Charles turns on voice-activated home lighting, Georgie Barrat heads back to 2000 - a time when tech was starting to take off despite a face-off with the millennium bug, and more money-saving tips from Harry Wallop

programme.title: Diana: A Mother's Love
programme.desc: An examination of how Diana, Princess of Wales, raised Princes William and Harry, focusing on her desire to keep their childhoods as normal as possible - in stark contrast to a family shackled by royal tradition. With contributions from members of Diana's household - butler Paul Burrell, bodyguard Ken Wharfe and private secretary Patrick Jephson - as well as biographer Andrew Morton and former royal correspondent Jennie Bond

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Bridget's focus on single life and her career is interrupted when she finds herself pregnant, but with one hitch - she can only be 50 per cent sure of the identity of her baby's father after a pair of one-night stands. Is the dad old flame Mark Darcy or US dating guru Jack Qwant? Comedy sequel, starring Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey, Emma Thompson and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Rom-Com
programme.desc: As St Valentine's Day approaches, a selection of film stars, comedians and TV personalities revisit some of cinema's most memorable romantic comedies, via a top 40 list interspersed with personal accounts of first loves. Celebrating the unique way this particular genre gets audiences laughing and crying in equal measure, and among the actors sharing their thoughts on the films are Sleepless in Seattle's Rita Wilson, Groundhog Day's Stephen Tobolowsky, Gregory's Girl star Clare Grogan and former child star Miffy Englefield, whose precocious antics in The Holiday helped Jude Law's widowed single dad win the heart of Cameron Diaz. Also includes contributions by Stanley Johnson, Sandi Bogle, Ricky Norwood, Kate Garraway, Bobby Norris and Love Island couple Camilla Thurlow and Jamie Jewitt
